rules: Check commands must pass before any add/writeback command is allowed.
Profile proof templates may pass structurally, but still require real external evidence before writeback.
Post proof templates must fail while placeholder URLs remain in place; after public URL writeback, KPI source proof is required.
A zero KPI is valid only after a real analytics source check.
Rehearsal data, sample URLs, and templates are never production evidence.

steps.release: Active profile proof templates validate and each active platform has six evidence checks.
steps.stop: Do not write profile status unless all six evidence checks are backed by real platform proof.

steps.release: Active post proof templates are safely rejected while their post URLs are placeholders; completed posts move to KPI source proof.
steps.stop: Do not weaken this guard; placeholder post URLs must never write to trackers or KPI rows.

steps.release: Profile import passes, placeholder post import fails, sample real post URLs pass, and operator import templates enforce safe rejection.
steps.stop: Do not run add/writeback commands until the check path behaves as expected.

steps.release: A real platform screenshot/click proof exists and the profile proof text passed check.
steps.stop: Do not write back from rehearsal data, templates, private previews, or memory.

steps.release: Profile writeback is complete and first-batch publishing is explicitly open.
steps.stop: Do not publish or backfill KPI while launch rehearsal says publish is blocked.
